text: the headline made everyone panic and then you read the article and realize losing #1 in a market that grew 40% means your slice is still bigger than last year. classic overreaction lol
author: fomo_king_420

text: wait so nvidia is #1 now but everyone's saying that's good for arista? i've only been following this stuff for like 6 months and this makes my head hurt. how is losing the top spot a positive??
author: noob_investor42

text: think of it like a pie that grew 40%. nvidia got a bigger slice but arista's slice is bigger in absolute dollars than last year. their revenue is still accelerating — that's the whole point of the article
author: thesis_driven_dan

text: can confirm from the inside — we're buying both nvidia and arista switches depending on the cluster tier. they coexist way more than the market thinks. ethernet migration from infiniband is happening faster than i expected honestly
author: tech_worker_42

text: $12B cash zero debt and 42.7% operating margins. name another hardware company with numbers like that. 63x trailing is steep but the balance sheet says blue chip not growth stock. weird disconnect
author: sharp315

text: i build AI training infra at a FAANG. can vouch for the 1.6T switches — they're real and they're going into production clusters right now. the article undersells how fast ethernet is eating infiniband's lunch in AI networking
author: ai_engineer_daily

text: lotta cope in these comments for a company that just lost the #1 spot. 63x forward PE for second place in a space where nvidia can reshape the landscape with one keynote. AVGO at 20x earnings looks way more interesting as a networking play
author: deep_value_diver

text: @deep_value_diver the total market grew 40% in one quarter. losing relative share in a market that's exploding =/= losing share in a flat market. arista's absolute revenue is accelerating. that's not cope that's math
author: thesis_driven_dan

text: $233B market cap on $9.7B revenue is 24x sales. even with the growth that's priced for perfection. one miss and it's a 30% haircut. the bull case works perfectly until it doesn't
author: Shdwrptr

text: the AI networking buildout is a multi-year cycle not a quarter-to-quarter trade. ethernet displacing infiniband across every hyperscaler is a structural shift. check back in 2030 and see who cared about market share in Q1 2026 🤷
author: CCWaterBug

text: the interesting question nobody's asking: if nvidia's spectrum-x is winning ethernet share, who's supplying the silicon? broadcom's tomahawk 6 is in arista's 1.6T switches AND nvidia's spectrum-x. hock tan wins either way lol
author: CarrotAwesome

text: oh damn @CarrotAwesome that's a good point. the picks and shovels play here is broadcom selling silicon to both sides of the fight. been sleeping on that angle
author: first_time_caller99
